1	\chapter{Relative Picard sheaf --- \texttt{Scheme.Modules.tensorObj} substrate (A.1.c.SubT)}
2	\label{chap:Picard_TensorObjSubstrate}
3	
4	% STRATEGY NOTE (iter-200). This chapter records the dedicated substrate
5	% on which the abelian-group instance of \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}
6	% (chapter \cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor}) rests. The genuine residual
7	% sorry at \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean} L235 is
8	% the abelian-group law on the relative Picard quotient
9	% \(\Pic(C \times_k T)/\pi_T^*\Pic(T)\); descending tensor product of
10	% line bundles to that quotient requires a tensor-product operation on
11	% the underlying carrier and an inverse for invertibles, neither of
12	% which is presently exposed at the \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\) level.
13	% Mathlib at the pinned commit ships
14	% \texttt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj} (a tensor product at
15	% the level of \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\)), but does not lift this
16	% to a monoidal-category structure on \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\). The
17	% chapter describes the substrate
18	% \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}.\mathtt{tensorObj}\), records the lift
19	% through the \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\) carrier used by
20	% \cref{chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback}, and pins the consumer
21	% instance back into \cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor}. The chapter is
22	% project-side and self-contained: dropping a Mathlib upstream PR is
23	% acceptable but downstream work does NOT wait for the PR to land.
24	
25	% NOTE (planner directive iter-200). The chapter has three
26	% responsibilities. First, it records what the Mathlib API surface
27	% currently exposes at the relevant abstraction levels, what is
28	% missing, and at which abstraction the project-side construction must
29	% land. Second, it spells out the construction of
30	% \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}.\mathtt{tensorObj}\) as a binary operation
31	% on the category \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), its functoriality, and
32	% the monoidal-category axioms it has to satisfy in order for the
33	% downstream Picard-quotient consumer to work. Third, it spells out
34	% the canonical lift through the \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\)
35	% carrier and pins the consumer abelian-group instance on the relative
36	% Picard quotient back into chapter \cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor}.
37	
38	\section{Motivation: the abelian-group-valued relative Picard sheaf}
39	\label{sec:tensorobj_motivation}
40	
41	Fix a base field \(k\) and a smooth proper geometrically integral curve \(C\) over
42	\(k\). In Kleiman's framework (\cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor},
43	\cref{def:rel_pic_sharp}), the relative Picard functor
44	\[
45	  \Pic_{C/k} \;\;\colon\;\; (\Sch/k)^{op} \;\longrightarrow\; \AddCommGrpCat,
46	  \qquad
47	  T \;\longmapsto\; \Pic(C \times_k T) \, / \, \pi_T^* \Pic(T),
48	\]
49	is, by definition, abelian-group-valued. The abelian-group structure on each
50	fiber \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) comes from tensor product of line bundles
51	(Stacks tag 01CR / Hartshorne~II.\S 6), and the structure descends to the
52	quotient because \(\pi_T^*\) is a group homomorphism.
53	
54	% SOURCE: [Kleiman], "The Picard scheme", \S 2 (FGA Explained Ch.~9 \S 9.2),
55	% Definitions df:aPf + df:Pfs (the absolute and relative Picard functors are
56	% abelian-group-valued; the relative one is defined as a quotient of abelian
57	% groups)
58	% (read from references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L1274-L1318).
59	% SOURCE QUOTE:
60	% "\begin{dfn}\label{df:aPf}
61	%  The {\it absolute Picard functor} $\Pic_X$ is the functor from  the
62	%  category of (locally Noetherian) $S$-schemes $T$ to the category of
63	% abelian groups defined by the formula
64	% 	$$\Pic_X(T):=\Pic(X_T).$$
65	% \end{dfn}
66	%  [\dots]
67	% \begin{dfn}\label{df:Pfs}
68	%   The {\it relative Picard functor} $\Pic_{X/S}$ is defined by
69	% 	$$\Pic_{X/S}(T):=\Pic(X_T)\big/\Pic(T).$$
70	%  Denote its associated sheaves in the Zariski, \'etale, and fppf
71	% topologies  by
72	% 	$$\Pic_{(X/S)\zar},\ \Pic_{(X/S)\et},\
73	% 	\Pic_{(X/S)\fppf}.$$
74	%  \end{dfn}"
75	\textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 2, Defs.~df:aPf + df:Pfs.
76	The absolute and relative Picard functors take values in the category of
77	abelian groups; the relative functor is the quotient of one such group by
78	another, and the structure morphisms of the resulting presheaf are group
79	homomorphisms.}
80	
81	Concretely, the abelian-group law on \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) is the assignment
82	\(\bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] + \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}'\,\bigr] :=
83	\bigl[\,\mathcal{L} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}} \mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]\),
84	with neutral element the class of the structure sheaf
85	\(\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}\) and inverse
86	\(- \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] := \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}^{-1}\,\bigr]\), where
87	\(\mathcal{L}^{-1} = \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}}(\mathcal{L},
88	\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T})\) is the dual invertible sheaf. Pullback of
89	\(\mathcal{O}\)-modules along \(\pi_T : C \times_k T \to T\) preserves tensor
90	products and the structure sheaf, so the image
91	\(\pi_T^* \Pic(T) \subset \Pic(C \times_k T)\) is a subgroup, and the quotient
92	\(\Pic(C \times_k T) / \pi_T^* \Pic(T)\) inherits a canonical abelian-group
93	structure. This is the content of
94	\cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid} in chapter \cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor}.
95	
96	The mathematics is straightforward; the obstacle to formalisation is purely
97	infrastructural. The Lean construction of the abelian-group law
98	\[
99	  \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] + \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]
100	  \;:=\; \bigl[\,\mathcal{L} \otimes \mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]
101	\]
102	on isomorphism classes of line bundles requires three ingredients on the
103	Lean carrier:
104	\begin{enumerate}
105	  \item a binary tensor-product operation
106	    \(\otimes\, : \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X \times \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X
107	                  \to \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\);
108	  \item the structure sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_X\) as a designated unit object
109	    for \(\otimes\);
110	  \item an inverse operation on the full subcategory of invertible objects,
111	    i.e.\ the dual \(\mathcal{L}^{-1} = \mathcal{H}om(\mathcal{L},
112	    \mathcal{O}_X)\) of an invertible sheaf.
113	\end{enumerate}
114	At Mathlib's pinned commit, only a presheaf-level version of (1) is
115	available; (2) and (3) are present as scheme-level objects, but the
116	binary operation in (1) that ties them together at the
117	\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\) level is missing. This chapter records the
118	project-side substrate that supplies (1) and consequently lifts (2) +
119	(3) into a monoidal-category structure on \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\).
120	
121	\section{Mathlib API survey}
122	\label{sec:tensorobj_api_survey}
123	
124	The relevant pieces of the Mathlib API at the pinned commit
125	(\texttt{b80f227}) split across two layers.
126	
127	\paragraph{Layer 1: presheaf-of-modules monoidal structure.} Mathlib's
128	\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.PresheafOfModules} module
129	constructs a monoidal-category structure on the category
130	\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R\) of presheaves of modules over a presheaf of
131	commutative rings \(R\); in particular, it exposes a tensor-product
132	operation \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\) realising the
133	sectionwise tensor product
134	\[
135	  (M \otimes_R N)(U) \;\;=\;\; M(U) \otimes_{R(U)} N(U)
136	\]
137	for open sets / objects \(U\) in the indexing site, together with the
138	associator, left/right unitors, and braiding making the resulting
139	category symmetric monoidal. This is the presheaf-level analogue of
140	Stacks tag 03DM (relative tensor product of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules).
141	
142	\paragraph{Layer 2: scheme-level \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules.} The module
143	\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) on a scheme \(X\) (the Lean target
144	expressed by the namespace \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules})
145	realises the category of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
146	on the small Zariski site of \(X\). It comes with pullback functoriality
147	(\(\mathtt{Scheme.Modules.pullback}\), used heavily in
148	\cref{chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback}), pullback comparison isomorphisms
149	(\(\mathtt{Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp}\),
150	\(\mathtt{Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr}\)), and the structure-sheaf
151	constant module \(\mathcal{O}_X\). It does \emph{not} ship a binary
152	tensor-product operation
153	\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X \times \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X \to
154	\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), nor a \(\MonoidalCategory\) instance.
155	
156	\paragraph{The gap.} The substrate \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}.\mathtt{tensorObj}\)
157	this chapter introduces is the named operation lifting
158	\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\) from Layer 1 into
159	Layer 2, packaged so that the consumer at chapter
160	\cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor} can read it as a binary operation on
161	\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}(C \times_k T)\) together with associator,
162	unitor, and braiding data. The project's policy on this gap is
163	\emph{project-side}: the substrate is built inside
164	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/} (a fresh file, sketched
165	\(\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean}\), is the
166	natural home) and the consumer instance is closed against it. A
167	parallel Mathlib upstream PR with the same content is welcome but not
168	on the critical path for the project; the consumer is closed against
169	the project-side substrate independently of any upstream timing.
170	
171	\section{The substrate \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}.\mathtt{tensorObj}\)}
172	\label{sec:tensorobj_substrate}
173	
174	\begin{definition}
175	
176	\leanok
177	[The substrate operation $\otimes$ on $\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X$]
178	  \label{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
179	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj}
180	  Let \(X\) be a scheme. For
181	  \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), define
182	  \[
183	    M \otimes_X N \;\;\in\;\; \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X
184	  \]
185	  as follows. Choose an affine open cover \(\{U_i = \Spec A_i\}_{i \in I}\)
186	  of \(X\). On each \(U_i\), the restriction
187	  \(M|_{U_i}\) corresponds (via the equivalence between
188	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\Spec A_i}\)-modules and \(A_i\)-modules) to an
189	  \(A_i\)-module \(M_i\), and similarly
190	  \(N|_{U_i}\) corresponds to an \(A_i\)-module \(N_i\). Define
191	  \((M \otimes_X N)|_{U_i}\) as the sheafification of the presheaf
192	  \(U_i \mapsto M_i \otimes_{A_i} N_i\). On overlaps
193	  \(U_i \cap U_j = \Spec A_{ij}\) the resulting modules agree via the
194	  canonical compatibility
195	  \((M_i \otimes_{A_i} N_i) \otimes_{A_i} A_{ij}
196	     \;\cong\;
197	     (M_j \otimes_{A_j} N_j) \otimes_{A_j} A_{ij}\),
198	  so the data \(\{(M \otimes_X N)|_{U_i}\}\) glue to a single object
199	  \(M \otimes_X N\) in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). The result is
200	  canonically independent of the affine cover chosen.
201	
202	  Equivalently: the underlying presheaf of \(M \otimes_X N\) is the
203	  presheaf-of-modules tensor product
204	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\) of the underlying
205	  presheaves of \(M\) and \(N\), composed with the sheafification
206	  functor on the small Zariski site of \(X\). The substrate operation
207	  \(\otimes_X\) is, by construction, a \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\)
208	  object reflecting the relative tensor product of
209	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules described in Stacks tag 03DM.
210	\end{definition}
211	
212	\begin{lemma}
213	
214	\leanok
215	[Functoriality of $\otimes_X$]
216	  \label{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}
217	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_functoriality}
218	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
219	  Let \(X\) be a scheme. The assignment
220	  \((M, N) \mapsto M \otimes_X N\) of
221	  \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} extends to a bifunctor
222	  \[
223	    \otimes_X \;\;\colon\;\; \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X \;\times\;
224	                          \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X \;\;\longrightarrow\;\;
225	                          \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X
226	  \]
227	  natural in both arguments: a pair of morphisms
228	  \(f : M \to M'\) and \(g : N \to N'\) determines a morphism
229	  \(f \otimes g : M \otimes_X N \to M' \otimes_X N'\) compatible with
230	  identities and composition. The functor preserves the structure
231	  sheaf as a unit, in the sense that there are canonical natural
232	  isomorphisms
233	  \[
234	    \lambda_M \;\colon\; \mathcal{O}_X \otimes_X M \;\xrightarrow{\sim}\; M,
235	    \qquad
236	    \rho_M \;\colon\; M \otimes_X \mathcal{O}_X \;\xrightarrow{\sim}\; M,
237	  \]
238	  and a canonical associator natural isomorphism
239	  \[
240	    \alpha_{M, N, P} \;\colon\;
241	    (M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X P
242	      \;\xrightarrow{\sim}\;
243	    M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P)
244	  \]
245	  and a canonical braiding
246	  \(\beta_{M, N} : M \otimes_X N \xrightarrow{\sim} N \otimes_X M\), each
247	  inherited from the corresponding data on
248	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal}\) via the sheafification functor
249	  of \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}.
250	\end{lemma}
251	
252	\begin{proof}
253	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
254	  Pick an affine open cover \(\{U_i = \Spec A_i\}\) of \(X\). On each
255	  affine, the assignment is the standard tensor product of modules over a
256	  commutative ring \(A_i\), which is bifunctorial, has the ring as a
257	  monoidal unit, and is associative and symmetric up to canonical
258	  natural isomorphism. These data glue across the cover because the
259	  underlying gluing data of \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} respect
260	  the canonical morphisms on overlaps. Equivalently, all four pieces of
261	  natural-isomorphism data (\(\lambda, \rho, \alpha, \beta\)) are
262	  inherited verbatim from the corresponding data on
263	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\) (Mathlib module
264	  \texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.PresheafOfModules}) under
265	  sheafification; the sheafification functor on the small Zariski site
266	  is symmetric monoidal because it preserves the relevant filtered
267	  colimits and finite limits used to construct the monoidal structure
268	  on \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\).
269	\end{proof}
270	
271	\begin{theorem}
272	
273	\leanok
274	[Monoidal-category structure on $\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X$]
275	  \label{thm:scheme_modules_monoidal}
276	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.monoidalCategory}
277	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}
278	  Let \(X\) be a scheme. The bifunctor \(\otimes_X\) of
279	  \cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}, with unit object
280	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\), associator \(\alpha\), left unitor \(\lambda\),
281	  right unitor \(\rho\), and braiding \(\beta\) of
282	  \cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}, satisfies the
283	  pentagon, triangle, and hexagon axioms of a symmetric monoidal
284	  category. Consequently \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) carries a
285	  canonical symmetric \(\MonoidalCategory\) structure.
286	\end{theorem}
287	
288	\begin{proof}
289	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}
290	  % NOTE (plan iter-204): there is deliberately NO proof-block  here.
291	  % The Lean instance AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.monoidalCategory has a
292	  % `:= sorry` body (intentional contamination guard; deferred-large monoidal
293	  % coherence, to be transported through the sheafification-forget reflective
294	  % adjunction). A proof-block  would assert "proof closed, no sorry",
295	  % which is false. The erroneous proof-block  that the deterministic
296	  % sync_leanok failed to remove across iter-203 was stripped here by the plan
297	  % agent as a correction of an over-claiming marker. The statement-block
298	  %  (the sorry-bodied decl exists) is correct and untouched.
299	  The pentagon and triangle identities (associator and unitor coherence)
300	  are inherited from the analogous identities on
301	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal}\). On any affine open
302	  \(\Spec A \subset X\), the assertions reduce to the analogous
303	  identities for the tensor product of \(A\)-modules, which are
304	  standard. The gluing data on overlaps is preserved by all four
305	  natural isomorphisms by construction, so the affine-level coherence
306	  identities yield global coherence identities on
307	  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). Symmetry of \(\beta\) follows by the
308	  same affine descent argument.
309	\end{proof}
310	
311	\section{The lift through \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\)}
312	\label{sec:tensorobj_onproduct_lift}
313	
314	The consumer in chapter \cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor} works not with
315	\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}(C \times_k T)\) directly but with the subtype
316	\(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) of locally-trivial
317	modules on the fiber product \(C \times_k T\), introduced in
318	\cref{chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback}. This section spells out how the
319	monoidal-category structure of
320	\cref{thm:scheme_modules_monoidal} restricts to that subtype, and how
321	the restriction interacts with the pullback functor \(\pi_T^*\).
322	
323	\begin{lemma}
324	
325	\leanok
326	[Tensor product of locally-trivial modules is locally trivial]
327	  \label{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}
328	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial}
329	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
330	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, and let
331	  \(L, L' \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) be locally-trivial modules of
332	  rank one (i.e.\ line bundles). The tensor product \(L \otimes_X L'\) of
333	  \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} is again locally trivial of rank
334	  one. Consequently the bifunctor \(\otimes_X\) restricts to a bifunctor
335	  on the full subcategory \(\texttt{LineBundle}\,X \subset \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\)
336	  of locally-trivial rank-one modules.
337	\end{lemma}
338	
339	\begin{proof}
340	  Pick a common affine open cover \(\{U_i\}\) of \(X\) on which both
341	  \(L|_{U_i}\) and \(L'|_{U_i}\) are free of rank one. On each \(U_i\)
342	  there are trivialisations \(L|_{U_i} \cong \mathcal{O}_{U_i}\) and
343	  \(L'|_{U_i} \cong \mathcal{O}_{U_i}\). The tensor product of two free
344	  rank-one modules over a commutative ring is again free of rank one;
345	  hence \((L \otimes_X L')|_{U_i} \cong \mathcal{O}_{U_i}\). So
346	  \(L \otimes_X L'\) is locally trivial of rank one, i.e.\ an object of
347	  \(\texttt{LineBundle}\,X\).
348	\end{proof}
349	
350	\begin{lemma}
351	
352	\leanok
353	[Inverse of an invertible module]
354	  \label{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible}
355	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.exists_tensorObj_inverse}
356	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}
357	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and let \(L \in \texttt{LineBundle}\,X\) be a line
358	  bundle. The dual
359	  \[
360	    L^{-1} \;\;:=\;\; \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(L, \mathcal{O}_X)
361	  \]
362	  is again a line bundle on \(X\), and there is a canonical natural
363	  isomorphism
364	  \(\varepsilon_L : L \otimes_X L^{-1} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_X\).
365	  Consequently every line bundle has a two-sided tensor inverse under
366	  \(\otimes_X\), and the monoid \(\bigl(\texttt{LineBundle}\,X / \sim, \otimes_X,
367	  \mathcal{O}_X\bigr)\) of isomorphism classes is an abelian group ---
368	  the Picard group \(\Pic(X)\) (Stacks tag 01CR).
369	\end{lemma}
370	
371	\begin{proof}
372	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}
373	  Locally trivial of rank one is preserved by
374	  \(\mathcal{H}om(-, \mathcal{O}_X)\): on a common affine trivialising
375	  cover, the dual of a free rank-one module is again free of rank one.
376	  The contraction map
377	  \(L \otimes_X \mathcal{H}om(L, \mathcal{O}_X) \to \mathcal{O}_X\) is a
378	  morphism in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), and on each open of the
379	  trivialising cover it specialises to the contraction
380	  \(A \otimes_A A \to A\) of free rank-one \(A\)-modules, which is an
381	  isomorphism; hence the contraction is a global isomorphism. The
382	  abelian-group structure on \(\Pic(X) := \texttt{LineBundle}\,X / \sim\)
383	  is then standard (Stacks tag 01CR).
384	\end{proof}
385	
386	\begin{lemma}
387	
388	\leanok
389	[Restriction to \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\)]
390	  \label{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}
391	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObjOnProduct}
392	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial,
393	        lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible}
394	  Let \(C \to S\) and \(T \to S\) be \(S\)-schemes and consider the
395	  \texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct} carrier on \(C \times_S T\) of
396	  \cref{chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback}, concretised as the subtype
397	  \[
398	    \texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T
399	    \;\;=\;\;
400	    \bigl\{\,M \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}(C \times_S T)
401	            \,\bigm|\, \mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\,M \,\bigr\}.
402	  \]
403	  The bifunctor \(\otimes_{C \times_S T}\) of
404	  \cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality} restricts to a
405	  bifunctor on \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) with unit
406	  the structure sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_{C \times_S T}\), and the dual
407	  \(L \mapsto L^{-1}\) of \cref{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible} is a
408	  two-sided tensor inverse on this subcategory.
409	\end{lemma}
410	
411	\begin{proof}
412	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial,
413	        lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible}
414	  Direct from
415	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial,
416	        lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible} applied to
417	  \(X := C \times_S T\): the tensor product of two locally-trivial
418	  rank-one modules is locally trivial of rank one, and the dual is
419	  again locally trivial of rank one. The unit \(\mathcal{O}_{C
420	  \times_S T}\) is locally trivial of rank one (trivially), so it
421	  belongs to \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\). The
422	  monoidal-category structure of
423	  \cref{thm:scheme_modules_monoidal} restricts to the subcategory by
424	  the same affine descent argument.
425	\end{proof}
426	
427	\begin{lemma}
428	[\(\pi_T^*\) is a tensor-functor]
429	  \label{lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj}
430	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}
431	  With notation as in \cref{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}, the pullback
432	  functor
433	  \[
434	    \pi_T^{*} \;\;\colon\;\; \texttt{LineBundle}\,T \;\longrightarrow\;
435	                          \texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T
436	  \]
437	  of \cref{def:pullback_along_projection} is a (strong) monoidal
438	  functor: there is a canonical natural isomorphism
439	  \(\pi_T^*(\mathcal{N} \otimes_T \mathcal{N'})
440	     \xrightarrow{\sim}
441	   \pi_T^* \mathcal{N} \otimes_{C \times_S T} \pi_T^* \mathcal{N'}\)
442	  for \(\mathcal{N}, \mathcal{N'} \in \texttt{LineBundle}\,T\), and a
443	  canonical isomorphism
444	  \(\pi_T^* \mathcal{O}_T \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_{C \times_S T}\),
445	  both inherited from the analogous data on the underlying
446	  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\) functors via the
447	  pullback-tensor-compatibility of \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules.
448	\end{lemma}
449	
450	\begin{proof}
451	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}
452	  On any affine open of \(T\) over which \(\mathcal{N}\) and
453	  \(\mathcal{N'}\) trivialise simultaneously, the assertion reduces to
454	  the standard fact that the extension of scalars
455	  \(B \otimes_A (M \otimes_A M')
456	   \;\cong\; (B \otimes_A M) \otimes_B (B \otimes_A M')\)
457	  for a ring map \(A \to B\) and \(A\)-modules \(M, M'\). This affine
458	  identity glues across an affine trivialising cover of \(T\) by the
459	  same descent argument as in
460	  \cref{thm:scheme_modules_monoidal}. The compatibility with the
461	  unit, the associator, and the unitors is verified pointwise on the
462	  same cover.
463	\end{proof}
464	
465	\section{Consumer: the \(\AddCommGroup\) instance for the relative Picard sheaf}
466	\label{sec:tensorobj_consumer_acg}
467	
468	The substrate of \cref{sec:tensorobj_substrate,sec:tensorobj_onproduct_lift}
469	supplies all the data missing from \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}'s Lean
470	encoding. The consumer instance below packages the abelian-group law
471	\(\bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] + \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]
472	 := \bigl[\,\mathcal{L} \otimes_{C \times_S T} \mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]\) on the
473	relative Picard quotient via the substrate.
474	
475	\begin{theorem}
476	
477	\leanok
478	[Abelian-group instance on the relative Picard quotient via \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}.\mathtt{tensorObj}\)]
479	  \label{thm:rel_pic_addcommgroup_via_tensorobj}
480	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.addCommGroup_via_tensorObj}
481	  \uses{thm:scheme_modules_monoidal,
482	        lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct,
483	        lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj,
484	        thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined,
485	        lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}
486	
487	  % SOURCE: [Kleiman], "The Picard scheme", \S 2 (FGA Explained Ch.~9 \S 9.2),
488	  % Definitions df:aPf + df:Pfs (the absolute and relative Picard functors are
489	  % abelian-group-valued; the relative one is defined as a quotient of abelian
490	  % groups)
491	  % (read from references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L1274-L1318).
492	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
493	  % "\begin{dfn}\label{df:aPf}
494	  %  The {\it absolute Picard functor} $\Pic_X$ is the functor from  the
495	  %  category of (locally Noetherian) $S$-schemes $T$ to the category of
496	  % abelian groups defined by the formula
497	  % 	$$\Pic_X(T):=\Pic(X_T).$$
498	  % \end{dfn}
499	  %  [\dots]
500	  % \begin{dfn}\label{df:Pfs}
501	  %   The {\it relative Picard functor} $\Pic_{X/S}$ is defined by
502	  % 	$$\Pic_{X/S}(T):=\Pic(X_T)\big/\Pic(T).$$
503	  %  Denote its associated sheaves in the Zariski, \'etale, and fppf
504	  % topologies  by
505	  % 	$$\Pic_{(X/S)\zar},\ \Pic_{(X/S)\et},\
506	  % 	\Pic_{(X/S)\fppf}.$$
507	  %  \end{dfn}"
508	  \textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 2,
509	  Defs.~df:aPf + df:Pfs (the target category is the category of abelian
510	  groups; the relative Picard functor is a quotient of abelian groups).}
511	  Let \(C/k\) be a smooth proper geometrically integral curve over a
512	  field \(k\), let \(\pi_C : C \to \Spec k\) be the structure morphism,
513	  and let \(T\) be a \(k\)-scheme with structure morphism
514	  \(\pi_T : T \to \Spec k\). The set
515	  \[
516	    \Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}(T) \;\;:=\;\; \Pic(C \times_k T) \,/\, \pi_T^*\Pic(T)
517	  \]
518	  of \cref{thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined} carries a canonical
519	  abelian-group structure with addition
520	  \(\bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] + \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]
521	   := \bigl[\,\mathcal{L} \otimes_{C \times_k T} \mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]\),
522	  neutral element \(\bigl[\,\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}\,\bigr]\), and
523	  inverse
524	  \(-\bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] := \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}^{-1}\,\bigr]\),
525	  where \(\otimes_{C \times_k T}\) and \((-)^{-1}\) are the operations
526	  supplied by the substrate of
527	  \cref{thm:scheme_modules_monoidal} restricted to the
528	  \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) carrier via
529	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}. With this structure the quotient
530	  map
531	  \(\Pic(C \times_k T) \twoheadrightarrow \Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}(T)\) is a
532	  surjective group homomorphism with kernel exactly \(\pi_T^*\Pic(T)\),
533	  in agreement with \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}.
534	\end{theorem}
535	
536	\begin{proof}
537	  \uses{thm:scheme_modules_monoidal,
538	        lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct,
539	        lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj,
540	        thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined,
541	        lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}
542	  By
543	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct,lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible},
544	  the substrate restricts to an abelian-group structure on the set
545	  \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T / \sim\) of isomorphism
546	  classes of line bundles on \(C \times_k T\). Concretely:
547	  associativity and commutativity come from the monoidal-category
548	  axioms of \cref{thm:scheme_modules_monoidal} (pentagon, triangle,
549	  hexagon coherence on isomorphism classes); the neutral element is
550	  the class of \(\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}\) (left/right unitor
551	  coherence); and the inverse is the class of
552	  \(\mathcal{L}^{-1} = \mathcal{H}om(\mathcal{L}, \mathcal{O}_{C
553	  \times_k T})\) (\cref{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible}).
554	
555	  By \cref{lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj}, the pullback
556	  functor
557	  \(\pi_T^{*} : \texttt{LineBundle}\,T \to
558	   \texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) is a strong monoidal
559	  functor; in particular it carries products to products and preserves
560	  the unit, so the induced map on isomorphism classes
561	  \(\pi_T^{*} : \Pic(T) \to \Pic(C \times_k T)\) is a group
562	  homomorphism. Its image is therefore a subgroup of
563	  \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\).
564	
565	  By \cref{thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined}, the underlying set
566	  of \(\Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}(T)\) is the set of right cosets of this
567	  subgroup. The quotient by a subgroup of an abelian group is itself
568	  an abelian group, and the quotient map is a surjective group
569	  homomorphism whose kernel is exactly the subgroup --- the standard
570	  fact (Stacks tag 01CR for the Picard-group special case; the
571	  general quotient-of-abelian-groups statement is in any algebra
572	  textbook). Putting the pieces together gives the
573	  \(\AddCommGroup\) instance on \(\Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}(T)\) of the
574	  statement, and identifies it with the structure of
575	  \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}.
576	\end{proof}
577	
578	\section{LOC estimates and prover-lane sequencing}
579	\label{sec:tensorobj_loc_estimates}
580	
581	The substrate splits into three logical pieces of roughly comparable
582	size; each is one prover lane in the iter-201+ schedule.
583	
584	\paragraph{Piece 1: definition + functoriality
585	(\cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj,lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}).}
586	The Lean target file is the new
587	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean}. The body
588	consists of:
589	\begin{itemize}
590	  \item a definition
591	    \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj} taking two
592	    \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) objects and producing one, via
593	    \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\) on the underlying
594	    presheaves and the sheafification functor on the small Zariski site;
595	  \item the functorial action on morphisms (bifunctoriality, identity
596	    and composition laws);
597	  \item the canonical natural isomorphisms (associator, left/right
598	    unitor, braiding) inherited from
599	    \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal}\) under sheafification.
600	\end{itemize}
601	Estimate: approximately 80--120 LOC project-side, split as
602	$\approx 30$--$50$ LOC for the object definition and gluing, $\approx
603	20$--$30$ LOC for bifunctoriality, $\approx 30$--$40$ LOC for the
604	natural-isomorphism data.
605	
606	\paragraph{Piece 2: monoidal-category instance
607	(\cref{thm:scheme_modules_monoidal}).} The same file, building on Piece
608	1. The body consists of:
609	\begin{itemize}
610	  \item discharging the pentagon, triangle, and hexagon axioms; each
611	    reduces affine-locally to the analogous identity on
612	    \(A\)-modules over a commutative ring \(A\);
613	  \item assembling the data into a
614	    \texttt{CategoryTheory.MonoidalCategory} instance with target
615	    \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), and into the symmetric variant
616	    (the hexagon for the braiding).
617	\end{itemize}
618	Estimate: approximately 60--100 LOC project-side, dominated by the
619	coherence-axiom discharge.
620	
621	\paragraph{Piece 3: lift + consumer

# Strategy

## Goal

Formalize Christian Merten's Jacobian challenge
(`references/challenge.lean.ref`): the nine protected declarations
headlined by `AlgebraicGeometry.Jacobian` and
`Jacobian.nonempty_jacobianWitness` — existence of an Albanese/Jacobian
object uniform over the `k`-rational pointing of a smooth proper
geometrically irreducible curve `C/k`, with **no** `C(k) ≠ ∅`
hypothesis. Char-general: `[Field k]` only, no `CharZero`.

The witness object `J` is always real (constructed unconditionally);
only `isAlbaneseFor` is universally quantified over `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C`.
Spine: **pointed vs. unpointed**. `genus C := dim_k H¹(C, O_C)`
(arithmetic genus; protected, cannot be re-typed).

**End-state — option (c), operative under USER 2026-05-28 ROUTE C
PAUSE.** The kernel-triple `#print axioms` contract cannot be delivered
unconditionally while every path to the protected decls transits A.2.c
(FGA Pic representability), which is RR-substrate-blocked. Operative
target: protected `#print axioms` verifies modulo the explicit residual
sorries in the post-Route-A cone audit, with consumers carrying the
RR-substrate dependency in commentary, not in the type signature.
Holds until the USER amends to (a) `J := Spec k` via Mumford rigidity
(carve `AbelianVarietyRigidity` + `RigidityKbar` out of the pause,
~300–500 LOC) or (b) full Route C re-engagement. Loop runs Route A
substrate continuously under (c); when all priority-1/2 roots close, a
TO_USER re-escalation surfaces the cone audit. Currently 0 project
axioms (24-consecutive-zero-axiom streak).

## Phases & estimations

| Phase | Status | Iters left | LOC (remaining · realized/it) | Key Mathlib needs | Risks |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| A.1.c.SubT — `Scheme.Modules.tensorObj` substrate | active; sole productive lane | ~2–4 | ~120–250 · ~2/it | `MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal` for the module-sheafification localization | Cone reduced to ONE gap (see Routes); `monoidalCategory := sorry` is a contamination-guard instance |
| A.1.c — RelPic functor | held (placeholder bodies) | ~3–5 post-SubT | ~50–150 · 0/it | A.1.c.SubT + ét-sheafify on `Over S` | RE-ENGAGEMENT GATE: must replace dishonest `PicSharp := const PUnit` first |
| A.4.a — codim-1 + Weil-divisor substrate | substrate DONE; terminal closure USER-blocked | ~0 active | ~40–80 · 0/it | terminal needs `[IsNoetherian X]`/`[CompactSpace X]` sig | sig strengthening is USER-gated |
| A.4.c.0 — codim-≥2 conclusion | **STUCK + PAUSED** pending USER | USER-gated | ~0/it sorry-progress | Step A2 = Stacks 02JK conormal-localisation iso | 27 iters flat at 3 sorries; "one more input" receded 4× |
| A.4.c.1 — Thm 3.2 assembly | priority-3 | ~8–14 | ~400–700 · 0/it | A.4.c.0 | re-engages iff COE L1262 closes |
| A.3.0 — tangent-space substrate | priority-3 (parallel) | ~6–10 | ~200–400 · 0/it | cotangent-at-id | shared substrate |

| A.3.vii–vi — degree map, Pic⁰ AV-structure | priority-4 (gated A.2.c) | ~20–35 | ~900–1700 · 0/it | Hilbert-poly decomp; tangent iso; smoothness; properness; geom-irred | scheme Hilbert poly absent in Mathlib |
| A.4.d.0/d — Pic^d + divisor-map Albanese UP | priority-5 (gated A.2.c + RR) | ~11–19 | ~400–800 · 0/it | Cartier divisor on `C × Pic^d`; RR genus formula | hidden Route C dependency |
| genusZero arm — `J = Spec k` | priority-6 (gated A.3.iii/iv/vi) | ~4–6 | ~200–400 · 0/it | tangent-iso + smoothness + connectedness | hidden A.2.c transit |

## Routes

The decomposition of `Pic` into open-closed pieces by Hilbert polynomial is RR-free (locally-constant Hilbert poly via flattening stratification); but *naming* the degree-0 piece and tying it to "degree" pulls in Riemann–Roch (`χ(L) = deg L + 1 − g`). RR enters only at (i) the degree/Hilbert-polynomial *identification* of components and (ii) dimension/divisor-map arguments downstream. 41	- **Sunk-cost reasoning detected**: borderline — "stay imported (no excision)" keeps ~9 sorry-bearing files in the build "modulo option (c)"; this is a "don't discard what we built" instinct rather than a merit argument, though excision would also be wasteful.
42	- **Infrastructure-deferral detected**: yes — this is the central finding (below).
43	- **Verdict**: CHALLENGE — the route's math is fine; the strategy must stop treating a goal-required construction as indefinitely pausable without surfacing that the goal is unreachable until it resumes.
44	
45	### Route: Genus-0 arm — candidates (a) Pic⁰-via-AV-wrap and (b) `J := Spec k` via Mumford rigidity
46	
47	- **Goal-alignment**: PASS — for genus 0, `C_{k̄} ≅ ℙ¹`, `Pic⁰` trivial, Albanese = `Spec k`; the universal property reduces to `Mor(ℙ¹, A) = const` (Milne Prop 3.10) + descent. Sound, and char-general (no Serre duality needed per Milne 3.2/3.10).
48	- **Mathematical soundness**: PASS — candidate (b) is the cheaper of the two and is correctly identified. The open question "may need only `AbelianVarietyRigidity`, not `RigidityKbar`" is well-posed: if the target is the *terminal* `Spec k`, the descended statement is a property (map is constant), so the descent is trivial and full k̄-rigidity for an arbitrary AV target may be unnecessary. The audit-against-Milne-§1 instruction is the right next step.
49	- **Verdict**: SOUND — but see the must-fix note that this arm closes **only** the genus-0 case; it does not advance the dominant genus ≥ 1 obligation, which remains RR-blocked regardless of amendment (a).

52	
53	- **Goal-alignment**: FAIL — the strategy states verbatim that "the kernel-triple `#print axioms` contract cannot be delivered unconditionally." The stated goal is the *unconditional* protected theorems. Option (c) — "protected `#print axioms` verifies modulo the explicit residual sorries … with consumers carrying the RR-substrate dependency in commentary, not in the type signature" — is a goal-weakening (sorry-conditioned closure) dressed as an end-state.
54	- **Sunk-cost / momentum**: yes — see sunk-cost flags ("24-consecutive-zero-axiom streak"; deferring TO_USER escalation until "all priority-1/2 roots close").
55	- **Verdict**: CHALLENGE — option (c) is a holding pattern, not an end-state. The only resolution that reaches the stated goal is (b) full Route C. The strategy should say this plainly and escalate the USER decision **now**, not after more substrate iters.

60	- **Headings**: PASS — exactly `## Goal`, `## Phases & estimations`, `## Routes`, `## Open strategic questions`, `## Mathlib gaps & new material`, in order. (The `## Reference index`, `## Blueprint chapters`, `## Focus questions` blocks were appended by the dispatcher's directive, not present in the file.)
61	- **Per-iter narrative detected**: yes — `"reduced to one gap (iter-204)"`, `"Lane COE STUCK/PAUSED pending USER (iter-204)"`, `"A.4.b (CLOSED iter-202)"`, and three `"USER 2026-05-28"` date-stamps. Iter numbers and event date-stamps are per-iter history that belongs in `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md`.

64	- **Format verdict**: DRIFTED — scrub the `iter-NNN` references and absolute date-stamps in-place; move the paused-file inventory to a sidecar.

67	
68	### Deferred: Riemann–Roch (Route C chain)
69	
70	- **Required by goal**: yes — the genus ≥ 1 case (the dominant part of the goal) runs through A.2.c `degComp` and the A.4.d divisor-map genus formula, both of which the strategy itself routes through RR.
71	- **Current plan for building it**: none with a project-side timeline — "PAUSED (USER 2026-05-28)", "Not budgeted; documented in the chapter files for future re-engagement."
72	- **Timeline**: absent.

Disposition note: the USER pause is a real external gate the planner cannot override — but the correct response is to surface the goal-unreachability to the USER *immediately* (the loop currently defers that escalation until "all priority-1/2 roots close"), and to stop presenting option (c) as a goal-achieving end-state. Also re-examine whether the RR coupling is genuinely necessary for *representability* (see A.2.c CHALLENGE) — part of this "block" may be self-inflicted by the degComp pivot.

79	- **What it looks like**: construct `Pic⁰` as the connected component of the identity in the group scheme `Pic_{C/k}` (open-closed for group schemes locally of finite type over a field, Stacks). This is purely topological/group-theoretic once representability is in hand — no degree map, no `χ(L) = deg + 1 − g`.
80	- **Why it might be cheaper or sounder**: it removes Riemann–Roch from the critical path entirely. The genus ≥ 1 obligation would then depend on representability (RR-free, see A.2.c) + identity-component (RR-free), unblocking the whole project from the Route-C pause.
81	- **What the current strategy may have rejected**: the strategy says the identity-component construction was "EXCISED from the critical path (rationale: `analogies/pic0-ker-deg-pivot.md`)". A fresh reader cannot evaluate that rationale, but the consequence — trading an RR-free construction for one that blocks the entire goal on a paused RR chain — demands an explicit re-justification.
82	- **Severity of the omission**: critical.

86	- **What it looks like**: prove `Pic_{C/k}` representable via Grothendieck/Nitsure Quot machinery (RR-free), then take the open-closed component containing `[O_C]` as `Pic⁰` (RR-free, via locally-constant Hilbert polynomial / flattening stratification — no degree identification needed).
87	- **Why it might be cheaper or sounder**: reaches a representable `Pic⁰` object without Riemann–Roch, i.e. *under the current Route-C pause*. It directly attacks the strategy's claim that A.2.c is wholly RR-blocked.
88	- **What the current strategy may have rejected**: unclear — the strategy asserts both representability routes need RR but does not separate representability from degree-identification.
89	- **Severity of the omission**: major.

93	- `Currently 0 project axioms (24-consecutive-zero-axiom streak).` — Why this is sunk-cost/momentum: the zero-axiom count is hollow while every protected decl still closes only modulo `sorryAx`, and a "streak" is a momentum metric, not a goal metric. Recommendation: report distance-to-goal (which protected decls are sorry-free under what hypotheses), not a streak.
94	- `Loop runs Route A substrate continuously under (c); when all priority-1/2 roots close, a TO_USER re-escalation surfaces the cone audit.` — Why this is sunk-cost/momentum: it keeps the loop busy on the sole productive lane and postpones the one decision (USER resolution of the RR pause) that actually gates the goal. Recommendation: escalate the goal-unreachability to the USER now; substrate iters do not change the blocking set.

97	
98	- `CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal.toMonoidalCategory`: VERIFIED (Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal.Basic).
99	- `CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal.instMonoidalCategoryLocalizedMonoidal`: VERIFIED — yields `MonoidalCategory` on the localized category from `[W.IsMonoidal]` + `[L.IsLocalization W]`.
100	- `CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal`: VERIFIED (appears as the `[W.IsMonoidal]` hypothesis of the above instances).

103	
104	- Infrastructure-deferral REJECT — **the strategy defers Riemann–Roch, which is required for the stated goal** (genus ≥ 1). Planner must (a) escalate the goal-unreachability to the USER this iter rather than after "priority-1/2 roots close", and (b) stop framing option (c) as an end-state.
105	- Route A.2.c: CHALLENGE — separate "Pic representability (RR-free, Grothendieck/Quot)" from "Pic⁰ extraction via degComp (RR-coupled)". The "both routes need RR" claim is over-stated and may be hiding an RR-free path to a protected-adjacent milestone reachable under the current pause.
106	- Route Main / Alternative "identity-component Pic⁰": critical omission — justify excising the RR-free identity-component construction in favor of degComp, the proximate cause of the global RR block, or re-engage it.
107	- Route TS / A.1.c.SubT: CHALLENGE (effort) — `120–250 LOC · ~2/it` is inconsistent with `Iters left ~2–4` (implies ~60–125 iters). Fix the estimate; the headline "Total Route A ~140–230 iters / ~4400–8200 LOC" implies ~30 LOC/it against a realized ~2 LOC/it on the only active lane — a ~15× optimism gap to reconcile.
108	- Parallelism under-exploited — A.3.0 (scheme-level tangent space, ~200–400 LOC) is an **ungated root** in the dependency graph yet sits at `0/it` while A.1.c.SubT is called the "sole productive lane". The self-contained lemma "connected étale group scheme over a field, dim 0 = `Spec k`" is likewise independent and idle. Open a parallel lane or record why these ungated roots are not being worked.
109	- Format: DRIFTED — remove `iter-204`/`iter-202` references and the three `USER 2026-05-28` date-stamps; relocate the paused-file inventory to a sidecar.
110	
111	## Overall verdict
112	
113	The route *architecture* is mathematically sound — `J := Pic⁰` as a base-point-independent witness with the Milne Albanese UP, and `J := Spec k` for genus 0, are the correct objects, and the one actively-progressing lane (TS monoidal) rests on Mathlib API I verified exists. But the strategy has a structural problem it is dressing as a holding pattern: **the strategy defers Riemann–Roch, which is required for the stated goal** in the genus ≥ 1 case, and adopts an "option (c)" end-state that it admits "cannot be delivered unconditionally." That is a goal weakening, and the loop's continuous substrate work — while forward-compatible — cannot reduce the blocking set, which is gated entirely on a USER decision the strategy postpones. Two specific challenges may partially dissolve the block: the "A.2.c needs RR" claim conflates representability (classically RR-free) with degree-identification, and the excised RR-free identity-component construction of `Pic⁰` was traded for the RR-dependent degComp that now blocks everything — both deserve explicit re-justification this iter. Effort estimates are internally inconsistent (60–125 implied iters labelled "2–4"; ~15× headline optimism against realized velocity), an ungated root (A.3.0) sits idle behind the "sole productive lane," and the document has drifted from the canonical skeleton via iter-number and date-stamp narrative. Verdict: CHALLENGE — proceed only after surfacing the RR goal-block to the USER and addressing the representability/identity-component challenges.
114

Strategy-critic returned **CHALLENGE** with must-fix items — I cannot silently ignore it. The most consequential are the mathematical challenges (#2 RR-free representability, #3 the excised identity-component `Pic⁰`), which the critic admits it raised *without* reading the pivot rationale. # Analogy: Pic⁰_{C/k} via ker(deg) instead of identity-component

## Mode
api-alignment

## Slug
pic0-ker-deg-pivot

## Iteration
197

## Question

Should the project pivot from defining `Pic⁰_{C/k}` as `IdentityComponent
(PicScheme C)` (Stacks 04KU/04KV, parked ~4 iters at 0/it) to defining
it as `ker(deg : Pic_{C/k} → ℤ_{C/k})` (A.3.vii, gated)?

## Project artifact(s)
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/IdentityComponent.lean:236-258` —
  `IdentityComponent` def (general substrate).
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/IdentityComponent.lean:737-743` —
  `Scheme.Pic0Scheme C` def (currently `sorry`; specialises substrate
  to `G = PicScheme C`).
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/IdentityComponent.lean:412-479` —
  `geometricallyConnected_of_connected_of_section`
  (Stacks 04KU helper; `sorry`-bodied, pending Stacks 037Q / 04KV
  substrate in Mathlib).
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/IdentityComponent.lean:779-784` —
  `PicScheme.degree` def (degree map on `k`-points, currently `sorry`).
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/Pic0AbelianVariety.lean` — downstream
  consumer; uses `Pic0Scheme C : Over (Spec k)` opaquely.
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/AlbaneseUP.lean:41-76` — further
  downstream consumer; uses `Pic0.jacobianScheme = (bundle C).scheme`
  with `Pic0Scheme` underlying.

## Decisions identified

The directive bundles two distinct decisions:

1. **What is `Pic⁰_{C/k}` definitionally?** Identity-component (Stacks
   04KU/04KV) vs degree-zero open-and-closed subscheme (Kleiman
   `thm:Pphifin` + `ex:curves`)?
2. **Is there a Mathlib idiom for "kernel of a group-scheme
   morphism"** that the project should use directly?

### Decision 1: Underlying scheme of `Pic⁰_{C/k}`

- **Mathlib idiom**: NEITHER `IdentityComponent` of a group scheme NOR
  `ker(deg)` of a morphism of group schemes exists in Mathlib b80f227.
  Mathlib has `MonoidHom.ker` (sets / `Subgroup G`) but no
  scheme-level `GrpObj.ker`. Mathlib's `CategoryTheory.Limits.kernel`
  requires `HasZeroMorphisms`, which `Scheme` does not satisfy
  (`Scheme` is cartesian-monoidal, not abelian). The **literature**
  has two equivalent definitions for smooth-proper-curve targets:
  Kleiman `prp:pic0` (identity component) and Kleiman
  `ex:curves` + Milne III.1 p.~87 (kernel of degree = `Pic⁰=Pic^0`).
- **Project's current path**: Identity-component via Stacks 04KU/04KV
  (parked, ~350 LOC pending Mathlib upstream `Stacks 037Q` /
  `04KV`).
- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost. The identity-component definition
  requires building project-side or upstream Mathlib infrastructure
  (`LocallyConnectedSpace` for locally-Noetherian schemes;
  `geometricallyConnected_of_connected_of_section`; descent of clopen
  partitions along fpqc base change). The degree-stratification
  definition only requires Kleiman §6 `thm:Pphifin` (the Hilbert-
  polynomial open-and-closed decomposition) and the curve-specific
  identification "leading coefficient of Hilbert polynomial = degree"
  (Riemann–Roch, RR.1).
- **Cost of divergence (current shipped path)**:
  - 2 typed sorries in `IdentityComponent.lean` (`geometricallyConnected_of_connected_of_section`,
    `IdentityComponent.baseChangeIso` iso slot) waiting on Stacks
    037Q / 04KV substrate not in Mathlib b80f227.
  - 1 typed sorry in `IdentityComponent.isSubgroupHomomorphism`
    needing EGA IV₂ 4.5.8 (smooth × geom-conn → conn) + Stacks 04KU.
  - 1 typed sorry in `IdentityComponent.isFiniteTypeGeometricallyIrreducible`
    needing the group-translation argument (EGA IV₂ 4.5.8/4.6.1).
  - Phase A.3.i parked at ~0/iter for 4 iters; ~350 LOC of
    Mathlib-upstream-grade work remaining.
- **Verdict**: **STRUCTURAL_OK** (pivot recommended).

### Decision 2: Mathlib kernel idiom for `f : G → H` of group schemes

- **Mathlib idiom**: No direct idiom. The category-theoretic shape
  ("kernel of a group-object morphism in a finite-limit category") is
  the pullback `pullback f (e_H ∘ !) : ker f ⟶ G`, where
  `e_H : 𝟙_C ⟶ H` is the identity of the target group object and
  `!` is the unique map to the terminal. Mathlib supports this
  IMPLICITLY via `CategoryTheory.Limits.HasPullbacks` on schemes, but
  does not package `GrpObj.ker` as a named construction.
  Closest precedents:
  - `MonoidHom.ker` (`Mathlib.Algebra.Group.Subgroup.Ker`): plain
    group homomorphism in Type, returns `Subgroup G`. Not applicable
    at scheme level.
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.ker`
    (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.IdealSheaf.Basic`): kernel ideal
    sheaf of a scheme morphism, i.e. `Y.IdealSheafData` not a
    `Scheme`. A different concept (set-theoretic preimage of zero
    section, not the categorical fiber over the identity element).
  - `CategoryTheory.Limits.kernel`: requires `HasZeroMorphisms`, not
    applicable to `Scheme` or `Over (Spec k)`.
  - `MonoidHom.fiberEquivKer`: the categorical insight (kernel =
    fiber over identity), but in Type only.
- **Project's natural reframing**: The directive's "Pic⁰ := ker(deg)"
  framing is conceptual. The PRACTICAL construction is the
  degree-stratification:
  `PicScheme C = ∐_{d∈ℤ} Pic^d C` (Kleiman `ex:curves`), with
  `Pic⁰ := Pic^0` the degree-0 open-and-closed subscheme. This sidesteps
  the categorical-pullback definition entirely.
- **Gap**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL (categorical `GrpObj.ker`) but with
  workaround: the degree-stratification gives the kernel directly,
  without invoking pullback-of-identity. The kernel is the union of
  connected components mapping to `0 ∈ ℤ`, and in the discrete
  codomain case this is just the preimage of an open-and-closed
  point.
- **Verdict**: **NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL** for the abstract idiom, but
  not on the critical path: the project does not need to invoke a
  generic kernel construction, only the curve-specific
  decomposition. The "ker(deg)" framing in the directive should be
  read as a NAMING / MATHEMATICAL JUSTIFICATION for the degree-0
  component, not as a literal `kernel.lift` / `pullback.lift`
  invocation.

## Confirmation of the equivalence "ker(deg) = identity component"

### Kleiman §6, `ex:curves` (lines 4665–4681 of `references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex`)

> *Assume $X/S$ is locally projective over $S$ and flat, and its
> geometric fibers are integral curves. Given an integer $m$, let
> $\Pic^m_{X/S} \subset \Pic_{X/S}$ be the set of points representing
> invertible sheaves $\mathcal L$ of degree $m$. Show the $\Pic^m_{X/S}$
> are open and closed subschemes of finite type; show they are
> disjoint and cover; and show that forming them commutes with
> changing $S$.*
>
> *Show there is no abuse of notation: the fiber of $\Pic^0_{X/S}$
> over $s\in S$ is the connected component of $0\in\Pic_{X_s/k_s}$.
> Show there is no torsion: $\Pic^0_{X/S}=\Pic^\tau_{X/S}$. Show each
> $\Pic^m_{X/S}$ is an fppf-torsor under $\Pic^0_{X/S}$.*

This is the **direct citation** for `ker(deg) = identity component` on
smooth proper geometrically integral curves: the degree-0 component IS
the connected component of the identity. The project's curve hypotheses
(`SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom + IsProper C.hom + GeometricallyIntegral C.hom`)
satisfy Kleiman's setup.

### Milne III.1 (PDF p.~93 / book p.~87)

> *Let $\Pic^r(C)$ be the set of divisor classes of degree $r$. For
> a fixed point $P_0$ on $C$, the map $[D]\mapsto[D+rP_0]:
> \Pic^0(C)\to\Pic^r(C)$ is a bijection **(both $\Pic^0(C)$ and

This is decisive. The iter-197 pivot analogy directly **answers the critic's challenge #3**: it cites Kleiman §6 `ex:curves` confirming that for smooth proper geometrically integral curves, `Pic⁰` (degree-0 component) **is** the connected component of the identity — they are the *same object*. The identity-component construction was excised not arbitrarily but because it needs clopen-descent / `LocallyConnectedSpace`-for-locally-Noetherian-schemes infrastructure absent from Mathlib (~350 LOC), whereas degComp needs only the Pphifin Hilbert-poly decomposition. So #3 is a rebuttal-with-citation, not a re-route.

But the critic's challenge **#2 has real substance**: the Pphifin decomposition is RR-free; only *identifying* the degree-0 piece by degree invokes RR. Taking the component through the trivial class `[O_C]` may yield `Pic⁰` RR-free. That's a genuine refinement worth validating (not unilaterally adopting on a fresh-context critic's word). Let me read the rest of the analogy to confirm the equivalence citation, then respond.

input.command: cd /home/archon/FormalizationProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge; sed -n '150,210p' analogies/pic0-ker-deg-pivot.md 2>/dev/null; echo "=== A.3.0 lean/blueprint readiness ==="; ls -la AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/ 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; grep -rl "Cotangent" AlgebraicJacobian/ --include=*.lean 2>/dev/null | head

content:
> \Pic^0(C)\to\Pic^r(C)$ is a bijection **(both $\Pic^0(C)$ and
> $\Pic^r(C)$ are fibres of the map $\deg : \Pic(C)\to\Z$)**.*

And p.~88:
> *We write $\Pic^0(C)$ for the group of isomorphism classes of
> invertible sheaves of degree zero on $C$.*

This is the **textbook definition of `Pic⁰` for a curve**: kernel of
the degree map. Milne's Theorem 1.6 then says this functor `P_C^0` is
represented by an abelian variety (the Jacobian), with no detour
through identity-component theory at all.

### Kleiman §6 `rmk:curves` (line 4682)

> *There is another important case where $\Pic^0_{X/S}=\Pic^\tau_{X/S}$,
> namely, when $X$ is an Abelian $S$-scheme. [...]*

Confirms the identification also holds in the abelian-scheme case
(used downstream when iterating to `Pic⁰` of `Pic⁰`).

**Conclusion**: The equivalence `ker(deg) = identity component` is
mathematically standard, explicitly cited in both primary sources
(Kleiman) and the project's secondary reference (Milne). For the
project's curve hypothesis it is provable from Kleiman §6 alone,
WITHOUT invoking 04KU / 04KV.

## Recommendation

**Pivot to ker(deg)-style Pic⁰ via the degree-stratification**, with
concrete plan:

1. **Repurpose `IdentityComponent.lean`** (or split into two files).
   The abstract `GroupScheme.IdentityComponent` substrate can stay
   for future use (no urgency to close its sorries — those are
   pure-Mathlib upstream-grade lemmas).

2. **Add Kleiman `ex:curves` decomposition** as the substrate.
   Sketch (using Mathlib b80f227 instances `inferInstance` shape):
   ```lean
   /-- Kleiman ex:curves: the degree-`d` component of the Picard scheme
   of a smooth proper geom integral curve, as an open-and-closed
   subscheme of `PicScheme C`. -/
   noncomputable def PicScheme.degComp {k : Type u} [Field k]
       (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
       [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
       [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom]
       (d : ℤ) : Over (Spec (.of k)) := ...

   /-- The open-and-closed inclusion `Pic^d_{C/k} ↪ Pic_{C/k}`. -/
   noncomputable def PicScheme.degComp.ι {k : Type u} [Field k]
       (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) [...] (d : ℤ) :
       PicScheme.degComp C d ⟶ PicScheme C := ...
   ```

3. **Redefine `Pic0Scheme C := PicScheme.degComp C 0`** in
   `IdentityComponent.lean` (or new file `Picard/Pic0ByDegree.lean`).
   The TYPE remains `Over (Spec k)`, so downstream consumers in
   `Pic0AbelianVariety.lean` and `Albanese/AlbaneseUP.lean` are

The pentagon and triangle identities (associator and unitor coherence)
  are inherited from the analogous identities on
  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal}\). On any affine open
  \(\Spec A \subset X\), the assertions reduce to the analogous
  identities for the tensor product of \(A\)-modules, which are
  standard. The gluing data on overlaps is preserved by all four
  natural isomorphisms by construction, so the affine-level coherence
  identities yield global coherence identities on
  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). Symmetry of \(\beta\) follows by the
  same affine descent argument.
input.new_string:
The pentagon and triangle identities (associator and unitor coherence)
  are inherited from the analogous identities on
  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal}\). On any affine open
  \(\Spec A \subset X\), the assertions reduce to the analogous
  identities for the tensor product of \(A\)-modules, which are
  standard. The gluing data on overlaps is preserved by all four
  natural isomorphisms by construction, so the affine-level coherence
  identities yield global coherence identities on
  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). Symmetry of \(\beta\) follows by the
  same affine descent argument.

  \emph{Concrete Mathlib realisation of this descent.} Rather than
  discharging pentagon/triangle/hexagon by hand, the coherence is
  transported through the sheafification localization. The category
  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) is the localization of
  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R\) at the morphism property
  \(W := J.W.\mathtt{inverseImage}\,(\mathtt{toPresheaf}\,R)\)
  inverted by sheafification --- the instance
  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.sheafification.IsLocalization}\,W\) is
  present in Mathlib. The generic transport
  \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal}\) (the instance
  \(\mathtt{instMonoidalCategoryLocalizedMonoidal}\)) produces a
  \(\MonoidalCategory\) on the localized category from a localization
  functor that inverts a \emph{monoidal} morphism property, i.e.\ from
  a \(\mathtt{MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal}\,W\) instance together with
  the \(\mathtt{IsLocalization}\) instance above. The single missing
  ingredient is therefore the \(\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\,W\) instance for
  the relative \(\otimes_R\) on \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R\)
  (Mathlib's existing \(\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) instances on the sites
  side concern the ambient/cartesian tensor on \(\mathtt{Sheaf}\,J\,A\),
  not the relative module tensor, so they do not apply). Once
  \(\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\,W\) is built, the same localization-monoidal
  transport simultaneously supplies the compatibility of \(\otimes_X\)
  with pullback/restriction (the strong-monoidal pullback iso
  \cref{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial} reduces to), so it is
  the highest-leverage single target for closing the whole substrate.

"24-consecutive-zero-axiom streak" momentum metric. All strategic content

## Decision made — strategy-critic clean205 response

I dispatched strategy-critic on the rewritten file (4 iters since the last
actual strategy dispatch + the COE-pause was a real strategic event). It
returned **CHALLENGE**. My disposition per finding:

  Goal end-state as a HOLDING PATTERN, not a goal-achieving end-state;

  The critic raised this without reading `analogies/pic0-ker-deg-pivot.md`.
  That iter-197 analogy cites Kleiman §6 `ex:curves` + `rmk:curves` and
  Milne III.1 (Thm 1.6): for a smooth proper geom-integral curve, the
  degree-0 component `Pic⁰` IS the connected component of the identity —
  degComp and the identity component are the SAME object. degComp was
  chosen because the identity-component construction needs clopen-descent /
  `LocallyConnectedSpace`-for-loc-Noetherian-schemes infrastructure absent
  from Mathlib (~350 LOC), whereas degComp needs only Pphifin. This is a
  cost argument over equivalent objects, not an arbitrary excision. The
  STRATEGY Routes section now states this inline with the citation.

- **#2 RR-free representability (CHALLENGE) — PARTIALLY ACCEPTED, elevated
  to a HIGH open question + iter-206 strategy-auditor.** The critic's core
  point survives: bare Grothendieck/Nitsure Quot representability is
  classically RR-free, and RR is only invoked when the degree-0 component
  is identified by its *degree* (RR.1). Picking the Pphifin component
  through the trivial class `[O_C]` (not "degree 0") may yield `Pic⁰`

  claim; I recorded it as a HIGH open strategic question and committed an
  iter-206 strategy-auditor validation against the primary PDFs (incl.

- **#4 TS effort inconsistency (CHALLENGE) — ACCEPTED.** The `· ~2/it`

# Iter-205 objectives (per-lane detail)

## Lane TS — `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean`

Mode: **mathlib-build**. Priority-1, sole productive Route A lane;
bottom-up spine TS → RPF → FGA → A.2.c.

**Mathematical content (reference-driven).** The category
`Scheme.Modules X` is the sheafification localization of
`PresheafOfModules R`. Mathlib supplies:
- `PresheafOfModules.sheafification.IsLocalization W` for
  `W := J.W.inverseImage (toPresheaf R)`
  (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.Localization`) [verified];
- `CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal.toMonoidalCategory` /
  `instMonoidalCategoryLocalizedMonoidal`
  (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal.Basic`) [verified] —
  yields `MonoidalCategory` on the localized category from
  `[L.IsLocalization W]` + `[W.IsMonoidal]` + a unit iso;
- `CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal` [verified].

**Single gap to build**: `MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal W` for the relative
`⊗_R` on `PresheafOfModules R`. The Sheaf-site `IsMonoidal` instances
(`Sites/Monoidal.lean`) are for the ambient/cartesian tensor on
`Sheaf J A`, NOT the relative module tensor — they do not apply.

**Ordered targets**: `IsMonoidal W` (axiom-clean) → `monoidalCategory`
(via the transport, drop the contamination-guard sorry only when fully
closed) → `tensorObj_restrict_iso` / `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`
(same transport supplies the strong-monoidal pullback) →
`exists_tensorObj_inverse` (Stacks 01CR dual + contraction) →
`addCommGroup_via_tensorObj`.

**Dead-ends (iter-204)**: `Scheme.Hom.resLE` not `homOfLE` for
unit-pullback chart-chases; pass `IsIso` explicitly to `@asIso`;
ascribe to `AddCommGrpCat` (not deprecated `AddCommGrp`).

**HARD BAR (mathlib-build invariant)**: `IsMonoidal W` axiom-clean OR a
precise axiom-clean decomposition of the obstruction — no sorry in output.

- COE — PAUSED pending USER escalation (Stacks 02JK Step A2).
- RPF / FGA — held (re-open post TS `addCommGroup_via_tensorObj`).
- WD terminal — USER-blocked sig strengthening.
- T32 — gated on COE L1262.
- Route C (RR chain), Lane RCI — USER-paused.
